mnemonics.title: PUE Formula
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility Energy ÷ IT Equipment Energy. Think: 'Pretty Useful Equation' - Higher PUE = More energy wasted on non-IT overhead.

mnemonics.title: Green Cloud Pillars
mnemonics.content: Virtualize, Consolidate, Cool efficiently, Use renewable, Monitor continuously. V-C-C-U-M.

mnemonics.title: Cooling Technologies
mnemonics.content: Free Cooling uses AIR, Liquid Cooling uses WATER. AIR+WATER = A+W = 'Away from waste'.

acronyms.usage: Measures overall data center energy efficiency; lower is better (ideal = 1.0)
Measures carbon emissions per unit of IT energy; indicates environmental impact
Inverse of PUE as percentage; higher is better
Energy-efficient storage with no moving parts
Isolated virtual computing environment running on physical hardware
Serverless computing model where code runs in response to events

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle representing total facility energy, then a smaller circle inside representing IT energy. PUE = big circle ÷ small circle. The gap between circles represents overhead (cooling, lighting).
Imagine one physical server shaped like a large pizza, cut into multiple smaller virtual pizza slices (VMs), each serving different customers independently.
VM = house with its own kitchen; Container = apartment sharing kitchen facilities. Containers are more compact and efficient.
Picture a data center with large vents (like a greenhouse) allowing cold outside air to flow directly through, with fans as backup.

analogies.analogy: PUE is like fuel efficiency of a car. Just as lower MPG means more fuel wasted, higher PUE means more energy wasted on non-computing activities.
Virtualization is like a multiplex theater - one building (physical server) showing multiple movies (VMs) simultaneously to different audiences.
Serverless is like hiring a taxi only when you need to travel - you pay for the ride (execution) not for the taxi sitting idle in the garage.
Edge computing is like having small local convenience stores vs centralized huge supermarkets - processing happens closer to where it's needed, reducing transportation (data transmission) costs.
